definition: A measurement of body weight relative to height, calculated as weight (kg) divided by height (m) squared. Used as a general indicator of whether someone is underweight, healthy weight, overweight or obese.

The three main nutrients the body needs in large amounts: carbohydrates (energy), protein (repair and growth) and fats (hormones and cell function). A balanced diet includes all three in appropriate proportions.

Any form of exercise that raises your heart rate and increases blood circulation. Examples include walking, running, cycling and swimming. Regular cardio improves heart health, burns calories and boosts mood.
A type of talking therapy that helps you manage problems by changing the way you think and behave. It is recommended by NICE for anxiety, depression and many other mental health conditions.
A blood test that measures your average blood sugar level over the past 2–3 months. It is used to diagnose and monitor diabetes. An HbA1c of 48mmol/mol or above indicates type 2 diabetes.

A condition where the body doesn't have enough Vitamin D, which is essential for bone health and immune function. Common in the UK due to limited sunlight, especially in winter. Symptoms include fatigue, bone pain and low mood.

The higher number in a blood pressure reading, measuring the pressure in your arteries when your heart beats. A normal systolic reading is below 130 mmHg. High readings (hypertension) increase risk of heart attack and stroke.

A set of habits and practices that help you get consistently good quality sleep. These include a regular sleep schedule, limiting screen time before bed, keeping the bedroom dark and cool, and avoiding caffeine late in the day.
